sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal.-

(1) to run, like the kindred שָׁקַק; whence שֹׁוק leg, and שׁוּק street.

(2) to run after, to desire, to long for any thing; whence תְּשׁוּקָה desire, longing. Arabic شاق to excite desire; V. to manifest desire; شَوْقُ desire.

Hiphil, to run over, to overflow (überlaufen); followed by an acc. of thing (with any thing), Joe 2:24, הֵשִׁיקוּ הַיְקָבִים תִּירֹושׁ “the vats overflow with must.”

Pilpel שֹׁוקֵק causat. to cause to overflow, or abound (used of the earth), Psa 65:10.

Derivatives, see under Kal No. 1, 2.
